1	package agent
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"encoding/json"
6		"fmt"
7		"io"
8		"regexp"
9		"strings"
10		"sync"
11		"time"
12	
13		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
14		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/config"
15		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/evidence"
16	)
17	
18	// ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime carries the non-serializable plumbing and
19	// per-invocation runtime intent for an execute-bead loop run. Durable knobs
20	// (assignee, retry caps, harness/model, tier bounds, etc.) live on
21	// config.ResolvedConfig and are passed via Run's rcfg argument.
22	type ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime struct {
23		Log          io.Writer
24		EventSink    io.Writer
25		ProgressCh   chan<- ProgressEvent
26		PreClaimHook func(ctx context.Context) error
27		// RoutePreflight, when non-nil, is invoked between nextCandidate and
28		// Claim. It is expected to call upstream ResolveRoute against the
29		// loop's resolved (harness, model) and return whatever typed routing
30		// error the upstream surfaced — notably agent.ErrHarnessModelIncompatible
31		// when the harness allow-list rejects the model. Any non-nil error
32		// causes the loop to exit immediately without claiming the bead, with
33		// a worker-level execution_failed record naming the rejected pair.
34		// DDx does NOT duplicate the upstream allow-list; this gate only
35		// consumes the typed-incompatibility surface.
36		RoutePreflight func(ctx context.Context, harness, model string) error
37		Once           bool
38		PollInterval   time.Duration
39		NoReview       bool
40		LabelFilter    string
41		SessionID      string
42		WorkerID       string
43		ProjectRoot    string
44	}
45	
46	// DefaultReviewMaxRetries is the number of reviewer attempts allowed per
47	// committed result_rev before the loop emits a terminal
48	// `review-manual-required` event and stops re-executing primary. FEAT-022 §14.
49	const DefaultReviewMaxRetries = 3
50	
51	// MaxLoopCooldown is the absolute upper bound the execute-loop will set for
52	// any execute-loop-retry-after value. Year-scale parks effectively mean
53	// "never retry" and that should be a deliberate operator choice via
54	// `ddx bead update --set execute-loop-retry-after=...`, not an automatic
55	// loop decision. Beyond this cap, the loop refuses to lengthen the cooldown
56	// further; an operator extending it manually still works.
57	const MaxLoopCooldown = 24 * time.Hour
58	
59	// LandConflictCooldown is the short cooldown applied when all conflict-recovery
60	// paths fail (3-way ort auto-merge + optional focused-resolve agent) and the
61	// bead is parked as land_conflict_unresolvable. Much shorter than
62	// MaxLoopCooldown because land conflicts often resolve quickly as sibling beads
63	// advance the base branch.
64	const LandConflictCooldown = 15 * time.Minute
65	
66	// StoreErrorCooldown is the short cooldown applied when a Store.* operation in
67	// the outcome-handling block returns a transient error. It prevents an
68	// immediate re-queue of the affected bead while allowing the loop to continue
69	// to the next candidate. Store errors during outcome handling are non-fatal:
70	// they are logged and emitted as loop-error events but do not terminate the
71	// worker.
72	const StoreErrorCooldown = 5 * time.Minute
73	
74	// handleOutcomeStoreError is called when a Store.* operation in the
75	// post-Execute outcome block returns a non-nil error. It logs the error,
76	// appends a kind:loop-error event to the bead (best-effort), sets a short
77	// cooldown so the bead is not immediately re-queued, increments
78	// result.Failures, and returns true — signalling the caller to continue to
79	// the next loop iteration rather than returning.
80	//
81	// If ctx is already cancelled, it returns false so the caller can propagate
82	// the cancellation immediately.
83	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) handleOutcomeStoreError(ctx context.Context, beadID, operation string, storeErr error, assignee string, result *ExecuteBeadLoopResult, runtime ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime, now func() time.Time) bool {
84		if ctx.Err() != nil {
85			return false
86		}
87		if runtime.Log != nil {
88			_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "outcome store error (%s %s): %v (continuing)\n", operation, beadID, storeErr)
89		}
90		_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
91			Kind:      "loop-error",
92			Summary:   operation + " failed",
93			Body:      storeErr.Error(),
94			Actor:     assignee,
95			Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
96			CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
97		})
98		cooldownUntil := now().UTC().Add(StoreErrorCooldown)
99		_ = w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(beadID, cooldownUntil, "loop-error", operation+": "+storeErr.Error())
100		result.Failures++
101		result.LastFailureStatus = "loop-error"
102		return true
103	}
104	
105	// capLoopCooldown clamps a loop-set cooldown duration to MaxLoopCooldown.
106	// The loop uses this for every SetExecutionCooldown call so no automatic
107	// path can silently park a bead for a year.
108	func capLoopCooldown(d time.Duration) time.Duration {
109		if d <= 0 {
110			return 0
111		}
112		if d > MaxLoopCooldown {
113			return MaxLoopCooldown
114		}
115		return d
116	}
117	
118	type ExecuteBeadReport struct {
119		BeadID    string `json:"bead_id"`
120		AttemptID string `json:"attempt_id,omitempty"`
121		WorkerID  string `json:"worker_id,omitempty"`
122		Harness   string `json:"harness,omitempty"`
123		Provider  string `json:"provider,omitempty"`
124		Model     string `json:"model,omitempty"`
125		// ActualPower is the routing-decision power of the model that actually
126		// served the implementer's call. Forwarded to the post-merge reviewer so
127		// it can request MinPower=actualPower+1 and bias routing toward a
128		// stronger reviewer (R4 pairing).
129		ActualPower int    `json:"actual_power,omitempty"`
130		Status      string `json:"status"`
131		Detail      string `json:"detail,omitempty"`
132		SessionID   string `json:"session_id,omitempty"`
133		BaseRev     string `json:"base_rev,omitempty"`
134		ResultRev   string `json:"result_rev,omitempty"`
135		PreserveRef string `json:"preserve_ref,omitempty"`
136		RetryAfter  string `json:"retry_after,omitempty"`
137		// NoChangesRationale carries the agent's explanation when status == no_changes.
138		NoChangesRationale string `json:"no_changes_rationale,omitempty"`
139		// ReviewVerdict is the post-merge review verdict (APPROVE, REQUEST_CHANGES,
140		// or BLOCK) when a reviewer ran. Empty when review was skipped.
141		ReviewVerdict string `json:"review_verdict,omitempty"`
142		// ReviewRationale carries the actionable reviewer-authored findings for
143		// non-APPROVE review outcomes.
144		ReviewRationale string `json:"review_rationale,omitempty"`
145		// Tier is the model tier used for the final attempt (cheap, standard, smart).
146		// Populated by tier-escalating executors; empty for single-tier attempts.
147		Tier string `json:"tier,omitempty"`
148		// ProbeResult is a brief summary of the provider health probe at attempt time.
149		ProbeResult string `json:"probe_result,omitempty"`
150		// CostUSD is the dollar cost of this attempt as reported by the harness.
151		// Tier-escalating executors propagate this so the escalation trace can
152		// compute wasted/effective spend.
153		CostUSD float64 `json:"cost_usd,omitempty"`
154		// DurationMS is the wall-clock duration of this attempt.
155		DurationMS int64 `json:"duration_ms,omitempty"`
156		// Profile routing telemetry. Populated when execute-loop uses a profile
157		// ladder rather than an explicit harness/model pin.
158		RequestedProfile string `json:"requested_profile,omitempty"`
159		RequestedTier    string `json:"requested_tier,omitempty"`
160		ResolvedTier     string `json:"resolved_tier,omitempty"`
161		EscalationCount  int    `json:"escalation_count,omitempty"`
162		FinalTier        string `json:"final_tier,omitempty"`
163		// DecompositionRecommendation carries the structured list of recommended
164		// sub-bead titles when Status == declined_needs_decomposition. The loop
165		// records these on the bead as a `decomposition-recommendation` event so
166		// operators (or helix-evolve) can split the bead without re-deriving the
167		// recommendation.
168		DecompositionRecommendation []string `json:"decomposition_recommendation,omitempty"`
169		// DecompositionRationale is a free-form explanation accompanying
170		// DecompositionRecommendation. Optional.
171		DecompositionRationale string `json:"decomposition_rationale,omitempty"`
172	}
173	
174	type ExecuteBeadExecutor interface {
175		Execute(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error)
176	}
177	
178	type ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error)
179	
180	func (f ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc) Execute(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
181		return f(ctx, beadID)
182	}
183	
184	// SatisfactionChecker evaluates whether a bead that returned no_changes is
185	// already satisfied and should be closed, or is still unresolved and should
186	// receive retry suppression. noChangesCount is the cumulative count including
187	// the current attempt.
188	//
189	// When satisfied is true the caller closes the bead with the returned evidence
190	// string as the detail. When false the caller applies a retry cooldown and
191	// leaves the bead open.
192	type SatisfactionChecker interface {
193		CheckSatisfied(ctx context.Context, beadID string, noChangesCount int) (satisfied bool, evidence string, err error)
194	}
195	
196	// SatisfactionCheckerFunc is a functional adapter for SatisfactionChecker.
197	type SatisfactionCheckerFunc func(ctx context.Context, beadID string, noChangesCount int) (bool, string, error)
198	
199	func (f SatisfactionCheckerFunc) CheckSatisfied(ctx context.Context, beadID string, noChangesCount int) (bool, string, error) {
200		return f(ctx, beadID, noChangesCount)
201	}
202	
203	type ExecuteBeadLoopStore interface {
204		ReadyExecution() ([]bead.Bead, error)
205		Claim(id, assignee string) error
206		Unclaim(id string) error
207		Heartbeat(id string) error
208		CloseWithEvidence(id, sessionID, commitSHA string) error
209		AppendEvent(id string, event bead.BeadEvent) error
210		Events(id string) ([]bead.BeadEvent, error)
211		SetExecutionCooldown(id string, until time.Time, status, detail string) error
212		AppendNotes(id string, notes string) error
213		IncrNoChangesCount(id string) (int, error)
214		// Reopen sets a closed bead back to open, appending notes to the bead's
215		// Notes field and recording a reopen event. Used by the post-merge review
216		// step when the reviewer returns REQUEST_CHANGES or BLOCK.
217		Reopen(id, reason, notes string) error
218		// Update mutates a bead in place. Used by the post-merge triage step to
219		// add labels (e.g. "needs_human") and metadata hints (e.g. tier-pin) when
220		// the triage policy escalates after repeated review BLOCKs.
221		Update(id string, mutate func(*bead.Bead)) error
222	}
223	
224	// readyDiagnoser is the optional interface the work loop uses to explain an
225	// empty execution queue. bead.Store satisfies it via ReadyExecutionBreakdown.
226	type readyDiagnoser interface {
227		ReadyExecutionBreakdown() (bead.ReadyExecutionBreakdown, error)
228	}
229	
230	// NoReadyWorkBreakdown explains why the execution-ready queue is empty when
231	// dependency-ready beads exist. Populated on an ExecuteBeadLoopResult when
232	// NoReadyWork fires and the store exposes ReadyExecutionBreakdown.
233	type NoReadyWorkBreakdown struct {
234		SkippedEpics       []string `json:"skipped_epics,omitempty"`
235		SkippedOnCooldown  []string `json:"skipped_on_cooldown,omitempty"`
236		SkippedNotEligible []string `json:"skipped_not_eligible,omitempty"`
237		SkippedSuperseded  []string `json:"skipped_superseded,omitempty"`
238		NextRetryAfter     string   `json:"next_retry_after,omitempty"`
239	}
240	
241	// ProgressEvent is the FEAT-006 structured progress event. It is defined
242	// separately in the server package (server.ProgressEvent); this alias lets
243	// the agent package emit events without importing the server package.
244	// The field names and types are identical — the server package deserialises
245	// these directly from the channel.
246	//
247	// Terminal phases: done, preserved, failed.
248	type ProgressEvent struct {
249		EventID   string    `json:"event_id"`
250		AttemptID string    `json:"attempt_id"`

1	package agent
2	
3	import (
4		"testing"
5		"time"
6	
7		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
8		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/escalation"
9		"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
10		"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
11	)
12	
13	// TestZeroConfigRetryEscalationPolicy verifies the retry/escalation policy
14	// composes correctly when the loop starts from the new metadata-driven
15	// "profile=cheap by default" path (commit 3ddf3d52, ddx-b2c9a245). It is a
16	// follow-up to AC6 of ddx-b790449b: confirm that transient failures retry
17	// within the cheap tier (no escalation) and substantive failures escalate
18	// cheap -> standard -> smart.
19	//
20	// The test drives a simulated tier ladder using the real escalation
21	// primitives (InferTier, IsInfrastructureFailure, ShouldEscalate,
22	// TierToProfile, BuildEscalationSummary). It does not require a running
23	// agent service or harness binary.
24	func TestZeroConfigRetryEscalationPolicy(t *testing.T) {
25		// 1. Unflagged bead: no tier label, no kind, short description.
26		// InferTier must default to cheap so the loop starts at the cheap
27		// tier instead of the previous always-cheap fallback or the SD-024
28		// "no profile" path.
29		b := &bead.Bead{
30			ID:          "ddx-zero-config-001",
31			Title:       "trivial cleanup, unflagged",
32			Description: "minor doc tweak",
33		}
34		startTier := escalation.InferTier(b)
35		require.Equal(t, escalation.TierCheap, startTier,
36			"unflagged bead must infer cheap tier (zero-config default)")
37		require.Equal(t, "cheap", escalation.TierToProfile(startTier),
38			"cheap tier maps to 'cheap' profile string consumed by LoadAndResolve")
39	
40		// 2. Transient infrastructure failure on cheap tier (e.g. 502 from
41		// the inference host). The policy is: do NOT escalate; defer with a
42		// retry-after and try the same tier again. The model wasn't given
43		// a fair chance, so escalation budget must not be consumed.
44		transientStatus := ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
45		transientDetail := "provider 502 Bad Gateway: connection refused"
46		require.True(t,
47			escalation.IsInfrastructureFailure(transientStatus, transientDetail),
48			"502 Bad Gateway on execution_failed must be classified as infrastructure failure")
49		require.True(t,
50			escalation.ShouldEscalate(transientStatus),
51			"execution_failed itself remains escalatable; the loop must consult IsInfrastructureFailure first to defer-vs-escalate")
52	
53		// Loop ordering invariant: when IsInfrastructureFailure is true the
54		// loop body defers (retry-after) instead of advancing the tier. The
55		// tier on the next attempt is therefore unchanged.
56		tierAfterTransient := startTier
57		require.Equal(t, escalation.TierCheap, tierAfterTransient,
58			"transient failure must not advance the tier ladder")
59	
60		// 3. Substantive failure on cheap tier (e.g. tests failed). Policy:
61		// escalate to the next tier (standard).
62		substantiveStatus := ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
63		substantiveDetail := "TestFoo failed: assertion mismatch"
64		require.False(t,
65			escalation.IsInfrastructureFailure(substantiveStatus, substantiveDetail),
66			"a model-capability failure detail must not match infrastructure patterns")
67		require.True(t,
68			escalation.ShouldEscalate(substantiveStatus),
69			"substantive execution_failed must escalate")
70		tierAfterCheapSubstantive := nextTier(tierAfterTransient)
71		require.Equal(t, escalation.TierStandard, tierAfterCheapSubstantive,
72			"cheap -> standard escalation step")
73	
74		// 4. Substantive failure on standard tier. Policy: escalate to smart.
75		standardStatus := ExecuteBeadStatusPostRunCheckFailed
76		require.True(t,
77			escalation.ShouldEscalate(standardStatus),
78			"post_run_check_failed escalates")
79		tierAfterStandardSubstantive := nextTier(tierAfterCheapSubstantive)
80		require.Equal(t, escalation.TierSmart, tierAfterStandardSubstantive,
81			"standard -> smart escalation step")
82	
83		// 5. Smart tier succeeds. Build the escalation summary and verify
84		// the full trail is recorded with winning_tier=smart and the cheap
85		// transient attempt counted as wasted-but-not-escalating-budget.
86		attempts := []escalation.TierAttemptRecord{
87			{Tier: string(escalation.TierCheap), Harness: "agent", Model: "cheap-model", Status: transientStatus, CostUSD: 0.0, DurationMS: 800},
88			{Tier: string(escalation.TierCheap), Harness: "agent", Model: "cheap-model", Status: substantiveStatus, CostUSD: 0.02, DurationMS: 1500},
89			{Tier: string(escalation.TierStandard), Harness: "codex", Model: "standard-model", Status: standardStatus, CostUSD: 0.15, DurationMS: 3400},
90			{Tier: string(escalation.TierSmart), Harness: "claude", Model: "smart-model", Status: escalation.SuccessStatus, CostUSD: 0.80, DurationMS: 9000},
91		}
92		summary := escalation.BuildEscalationSummary(attempts, string(escalation.TierSmart))
93		require.Equal(t, string(escalation.TierSmart), summary.WinningTier,
94			"smart tier won the escalation")
95		require.Len(t, summary.TiersAttempted, 4,
96			"trail records all attempts including the deferred-transient cheap attempt")
97		assert.InDelta(t, 0.97, summary.TotalCostUSD, 1e-9)
98		assert.InDelta(t, 0.17, summary.WastedCostUSD, 1e-9,
99			"cheap-substantive (0.02) + standard (0.15) wasted; cheap-transient billed $0; smart succeeded")
100		assert.Equal(t, string(escalation.TierCheap), summary.TiersAttempted[0].Tier,
101			"first attempt was cheap (the new metadata-driven default)")
102		assert.Equal(t, string(escalation.TierSmart), summary.TiersAttempted[3].Tier,
103			"final attempt was smart (top of ladder)")
104	}
105	
106	// TestZeroConfigEscalationSummaryEventBody verifies the escalation summary
107	// is emitted as a kind:escalation-summary bead event when the cheap-default
108	// ladder runs to completion. This is the operator-visible artifact of the
109	// retry/escalation policy.
110	func TestZeroConfigEscalationSummaryEventBody(t *testing.T) {
111		store := bead.NewStore(t.TempDir())
112		require.NoError(t, store.Init())
113		target := &bead.Bead{ID: "ddx-zero-config-002", Title: "cheap-default ladder", Priority: 0}
114		require.NoError(t, store.Create(target))
115	
116		attempts := []escalation.TierAttemptRecord{
117			{Tier: string(escalation.TierCheap), Status: ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed, CostUSD: 0.01, DurationMS: 1000},
118			{Tier: string(escalation.TierStandard), Status: ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed, CostUSD: 0.10, DurationMS: 2000},
119			{Tier: string(escalation.TierSmart), Status: escalation.SuccessStatus, CostUSD: 0.50, DurationMS: 4000},
120		}
121		require.NoError(t,
122			escalation.AppendEscalationSummaryEvent(
123				store, target.ID, "test-worker", attempts, string(escalation.TierSmart), time.Unix(1, 0).UTC()))
124	
125		events, err := store.Events(target.ID)
126		require.NoError(t, err)
127		var found *bead.BeadEvent
128		for i := range events {
129			if events[i].Kind == "escalation-summary" {
130				found = &events[i]
131				break
132			}
133		}
134		require.NotNil(t, found, "escalation-summary event must be appended for the cheap-default ladder run")
135		assert.Contains(t, found.Summary, "winning_tier=smart")
136		assert.Contains(t, found.Summary, "attempts=3")
137		assert.Contains(t, found.Body, `"tier":"cheap"`,
138			"summary body records cheap as the first-attempted tier (zero-config default)")
139		assert.Contains(t, found.Body, `"tier":"standard"`)
140		assert.Contains(t, found.Body, `"tier":"smart"`)
141	}
142	
143	// nextTier advances one rung up the cheap -> standard -> smart ladder.
144	// Mirrors the implicit ladder used by the execute-loop when escalating
145	// beyond a substantive failure. Smart is the top; further calls stay
146	// at smart (callers exhaust escalation when ShouldEscalate fires at smart).
147	func nextTier(t escalation.ModelTier) escalation.ModelTier {
148		switch t {
149		case escalation.TierCheap:
150			return escalation.TierStandard
151		case escalation.TierStandard:
152			return escalation.TierSmart
153		default:
154			return escalation.TierSmart
155		}
156	}

60	// Permissions, Timeout, WallClock, SessionLogDir) are stripped — they
61	// live on config.ResolvedConfig and are passed via RunWithConfig's
62	// rcfg argument. Only non-serializable plumbing and per-invocation
63	// runtime intent remain.
64	//
65	// See SD-024 / TD-024 §Runtime structs and §Stage 2 bead 17.
66	type AgentRunRuntime struct {
67		Prompt                string
68		PromptFile            string
69		PromptSource          string
70		Output                io.Writer
71		Correlation           map[string]string
72		Record                bool
73		Replay                bool
74		WorkDir               string
75		EstimatedPromptTokens int
76		RequiresTools         bool
77		SessionLogDirOverride string
78		// HarnessOverride, ModelOverride, and PermissionsOverride let a caller
79		// pin one of the durable knobs from rcfg for this single invocation
80		// without re-resolving the full ResolvedConfig. SD-024 step B22d-d:
81		// execute-bead pins permissions=unrestricted; the post-merge reviewer
82		// pins harness/model to the reviewer-tier selection.
83		HarnessOverride     string
84		ModelOverride       string
85		PermissionsOverride string
86		// MinPowerOverride, when > 0, overrides rcfg.MinPower() for this single
87		// invocation. Used by the post-merge reviewer to pin MinPower above the
88		// implementer's actual selected power, biasing routing toward a stronger
89		// reviewer model (R4 pairing: reviewer >= impl + 1 power).
90		MinPowerOverride int
91	}

93	// Result holds the output of an agent invocation.
94	type Result struct {
95		Harness         string `json:"harness"`
96		Provider        string `json:"provider,omitempty"`
97		Model           string `json:"model,omitempty"`

99		ExitCode        int    `json:"exit_code"`
100		Output          string `json:"output"`
101		CondensedOutput string `json:"condensed_output,omitempty"`
102		Stderr          string `json:"stderr,omitempty"`
103		// Routing evidence populated by the embedded agent harness (RunAgent) or
104		// the script harness (RunScript). Used by ExecuteBead to record kind:routing
105		// evidence on the bead.
106		RouteReason     string          `json:"route_reason,omitempty"`
107		ResolvedBaseURL string          `json:"resolved_base_url,omitempty"`
108		Tokens          int             `json:"tokens,omitempty"`
109		InputTokens     int             `json:"input_tokens,omitempty"`
110		OutputTokens    int             `json:"output_tokens,omitempty"`
111		CostUSD         float64         `json:"cost_usd,omitempty"`
112		DurationMS      int             `json:"duration_ms"`
113		Error           string          `json:"error,omitempty"`
114		ToolCalls       []ToolCallEntry `json:"tool_calls,omitempty"`       // populated by agent, nil for subprocess
115		AgentSessionID  string          `json:"agent_session_id,omitempty"` // agent session ID for event log cross-reference
116	}

134		NativeSessionID string            `json:"native_session_id,omitempty"`
135		NativeLogRef    string            `json:"native_log_ref,omitempty"`

148		ResultRev       string            `json:"result_rev,omitempty"`   // git SHA of landed/preserved iteration (execute-bead only)

150	}

1	package agent
2	
3	// service_run.go provides Runner-free helpers that drive every operation via
4	// the agentlib.FizeauService service surface (CONTRACT-003). RunWithConfigViaService
5	// is the only run entry point; legacy RunViaService/RunViaServiceWith and the
6	// runFixtureHarnessViaRunner shim were retired in B22d-h.
7	
8	import (
9		"context"
10		"fmt"
11		"os"
12		"strings"
13		"time"
14	
15		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/config"
16		agentlib "github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/fizeau"
17	)
18	
19	// appendProviderTimeoutHint appends a configuration override hint to errMsg
20	// when it indicates a provider request timeout fired. Helps operators find
21	// the knob to adjust (AC4 of ddx-2c63bb95).
22	func appendProviderTimeoutHint(errMsg string, providerTimeout time.Duration) string {
23		if errMsg == "" || !strings.Contains(errMsg, "provider request timeout") {
24			return errMsg
25		}
26		return errMsg + fmt.Sprintf(
27			"; request_timeout=%s exceeded — override via"+
28				" agent.endpoints.<name>.request_timeout_seconds in .ddx/config.yaml"+
29				" or pass --request-timeout DURATION to execute-bead/execute-loop",
30			providerTimeout.Round(time.Second),
31		)
32	}
33	
34	// serviceRunFactory, when non-nil, overrides NewServiceFromWorkDir inside
35	// RunWithConfigViaService. CLI-level integration tests inject a stub to
36	// observe ServiceExecuteRequest fields without a real agent server.
37	var serviceRunFactory func(workDir string) (agentlib.FizeauService, error)
38	
39	// SetServiceRunFactory installs a service factory for RunWithConfigViaService.
40	// Pass nil to restore production behavior. Exported for cmd/ integration tests.
41	func SetServiceRunFactory(f func(workDir string) (agentlib.FizeauService, error)) {
42		serviceRunFactory = f
43	}
44	
45	// RunWithConfigViaService dispatches a single agent invocation through the
46	// agent service. It takes a sealed ResolvedConfig (durable knobs) and an
47	// AgentRunRuntime (per-invocation plumbing/intent), and is the SD-024
48	// successor to the retired RunViaService/RunViaServiceWith entry points.
49	//
50	// The virtual and script harnesses route through a DDx-owned Runner path,
51	// not the upstream service. These are not "carve-outs pending migration" —
52	// they are different products from upstream's same-named stubs. DDx's
53	// virtual is a content-addressed record/replay dictionary keyed by
54	// PromptHash; upstream's is a unit-test stub where callers stuff
55	// virtual.response into Metadata. DDx's script reads a filesystem directive
56	// file; upstream does not model this at all.
57	func RunWithConfigViaService(ctx context.Context, workDir string, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, runtime AgentRunRuntime) (*Result, error) {
58		if ctx == nil {
59			ctx = context.Background()
60		}
61	
62		harness := rcfg.Passthrough().Harness
63	
64		if harness == "virtual" || harness == "script" {
65			sessionLogDir := runtime.SessionLogDirOverride
66			if sessionLogDir == "" {
67				sessionLogDir = rcfg.SessionLogDir()
68			}
69			cfg := Config{SessionLogDir: ResolveLogDir(workDir, "")}
70			r := NewRunner(cfg)
71			r.WorkDir = workDir
72			return r.runInternal(RunArgs{
73				Context:       ctx,
74				Harness:       rcfg.Harness(),
75				Prompt:        runtime.Prompt,
76				PromptFile:    runtime.PromptFile,
77				PromptSource:  runtime.PromptSource,
78				Correlation:   runtime.Correlation,
79				Model:         rcfg.Model(),
80				Provider:      rcfg.Provider(),
81				ModelRef:      rcfg.ModelRef(),
82				Effort:        rcfg.Effort(),
83				Timeout:       rcfg.Timeout(),
84				WallClock:     rcfg.WallClock(),
85				WorkDir:       runtime.WorkDir,
86				Permissions:   rcfg.Permissions(),
87				SessionLogDir: sessionLogDir,
88			})
89		}
90	
91		factory := serviceRunFactory
92		if factory == nil {
93			factory = NewServiceFromWorkDir
94		}
95		svc, err := factory(workDir)
96		if err != nil {
97			return nil, fmt.Errorf("agent: build service: %w", err)
98		}
99		return executeOnService(ctx, svc, workDir, rcfg, runtime)
100	}
101	
102	// executeOnService dispatches against a pre-built svc using the durable
103	// knobs from rcfg and per-invocation plumbing from runtime, then records
104	// one session-index row. It is the inlined replacement for the retired
105	// RunViaServiceWith helper and is shared with dispatchViaResolvedConfig.
106	func executeOnService(ctx context.Context, svc agentlib.FizeauService, workDir string, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, runtime AgentRunRuntime) (*Result, error) {
107		if svc == nil {
108			return nil, fmt.Errorf("agent: nil service")
109		}
110		if ctx == nil {
111			ctx = context.Background()
112		}
113	
114		promptText := runtime.Prompt
115		if runtime.PromptFile != "" {
116			data, err := readPromptFileBounded(runtime.PromptFile)
117			if err != nil {
118				return nil, fmt.Errorf("agent: read prompt file: %w", err)
119			}
120			promptText = string(data)
121		}
122		if promptText == "" {
123			return nil, fmt.Errorf("agent: prompt is required")
124		}
125	
126		wd := runtime.WorkDir
127		if wd == "" {
128			wd = workDir
129		}
130		if wd == "" {
131			wd, _ = os.Getwd()
132		}
133	
134		idle := rcfg.Timeout()
135		if idle <= 0 {
136			idle = time.Duration(DefaultTimeoutMS) * time.Millisecond
137		}
138		wall := rcfg.WallClock()
139		if wall <= 0 {
140			wall = time.Duration(DefaultWallClockMS) * time.Millisecond
141		}
142	
143		sessionLogDir := runtime.SessionLogDirOverride
144		if sessionLogDir == "" {
145			sessionLogDir = rcfg.SessionLogDir()
146		}
147		if sessionLogDir == "" {
148			sessionLogDir = ResolveLogDir(workDir, "")
149		}
150	
151		pt := rcfg.Passthrough()
152	
153		harness := runtime.HarnessOverride
154		if harness == "" {
155			harness = pt.Harness
156		}
157	
158		model := runtime.ModelOverride
159		if model == "" {
160			model = pt.Model
161		}
162	
163		permissions := runtime.PermissionsOverride
164		if permissions == "" {
165			permissions = rcfg.Permissions()
166		}
167	
168		providerTimeout := ResolveProviderRequestTimeout(workDir, pt.Provider, model, rcfg.ProviderRequestTimeout())
169	
170		minPower := rcfg.MinPower()
171		if runtime.MinPowerOverride > 0 {
172			minPower = runtime.MinPowerOverride
173		}
174	
175		req := agentlib.ServiceExecuteRequest{
176			Prompt:          promptText,
177			Model:           model,
